title: pyrus horti-edeni
side_effects: Pyrus horti-edeni can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high levels of amygdalin, a compound that releases cyanide when ingested. In severe cases, overconsumption of the plant's extract can lead to respiratory failure, seizures, and even death. Additionally, improper use of Pyrus horti-edeni can also cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using any plant-based remedies to avoid these potential side effects.
